properties.NAME: Dardagani
properties.LOCATION: 5 km north of Zvornik, Bosnia and Herzegovina
properties.PRODUCTS: Sarcophagi, stelae, medallions, votive altars, architectural elements
properties.BIBLIOGRAPHY: Rižnar and Jovanović 2006, Djurić et al. 2006; 2007; 2012;  Russell 2013c; Ostojić et al. (forthcoming).
properties.UNFINISHED_PRODUCTS:
Ostjenak: The Roman quarry dump in a part of Locus II revealed fragments of roughed-out products, mostly sarcophagi, which were made on site.
Novi Izvor, Galleries I – III: The roughed-out products were found in the quarry, mostly sarcophagi. These bear marks of a toothed chisel and serve as evidence of production on the site of extraction. In Gallery I was found also an unfinished sarcophagus made of white fine grained porous limestone (Lithotype III), which confirm that also an ‘unpure’ (transitional) type of limestone was used.
properties.TOOLS: Two iron wedges were found in the underground part of the Bandera quarry and are now kept in a local collection at Zvornik. They correspond to the preserved wedge holes in both size and form.
properties.MEASUREMENTS:
Novi Izvor, Galleries I-III: the largest is Gallery I, which measures roughly 100 m in width and 90 m in depth. Gallery II is estimated to measure 40 m in width and 30 m in depth, Gallery III is privately owned and closed off. All three galleries have loci measuring up to 10 x 12 m in plan and up to 4 m in height.  Surface extraction can further be supposed in lengths of 270 m on the southern slopes of Bandera Hill.
Sige: 370 x 400 m or more; that the area of Roman extraction extended from the slopes of the Ostjenak Hill, to the north, to the end of the western slopes of the Bandera Hill.

properties.NAME: Rajići
properties.LOCATION: 23 km south of Zvornik, Bosnia and Herzeovina
properties.COMMENTS: Roman exploitation of Rajići quarry is confirmed based on the distinctive volcaniclastic rock which has been used for Sirmium sarcophagi; however, the exact area of Roman exploitation is not yet known. Quarry is still exploited today.

properties.NAME: Medvednica Mountain
properties.SUPPLY: Andautonia, blocks probably transported down to Siscia
properties.ARCHAEOLOGICAL_EVIDENCE: Altars at quarry sites, report also: unfinished columns and iron tools
properties.BIBLIOGRAPHY: Brunšmid 1907; Degmedžić 1957, 16, 21-22; 1909; Galić and Radman-Livaja 2002; Knezovič 2012, 240; Migotti et al. 2018, 206-208.
properties.UNFINISHED_PRODUCTS: Unfinished column found at the Vrapče site (today lost), and at the Crna voda site was found altar to Jupiter together with undressed stones.
properties.TOOLS: Several items of Roman mining tools were found at Vrapče site, which are all lost today.
properties.COMMENTS: In 1894 at the Vrapče site was found altar to Silvanus.

properties.NAME: Hrastovica
properties.UNFINISHED_PRODUCTS: Fragmented coffin was found at the Bijele Stijene site, measuring approximately 1 x 1,5 m and seeming to be roughed-out or an unfinished discarded piece which should have been carved into sarcophagus or ash-chest.
properties.COMMENTS: At the Bijele Stijene site, surfaces do not have any traces of quarrying, but there are some larger stone blocks scattered in clusters at their foot.

properties.NAME: Topusko
properties.LOCATION: Between Petrova and Zrinjska Mountain, Croatia
properties.ARCHAEOLOGICAL_EVIDENCE: Obliterated traces, the only remains of a former quarry cut into a hill slope are two lumpy rocks
properties.COMMENTS:
Roman traces at the Draškovičevi podrumi site have been obliterated during the construction of cellars cut into a slope in the 18th century.
The only remains of a former quarry cut into a hill slope are two lumpy rocks at a small distance to each other at the Žuti kamen site.

properties.NAME: Region Kordun
properties.LOCATION: Region in Croatia, on map Slunj town as the centre of the region, Croatia
properties.ARCHAEOLOGICAL_EVIDENCE: 37 Roman quarries documented, sarcophagi and urns in various stages of production
properties.POTENTIAL_TRANSPORT: Korana (2 sarcophagi), Mrežnica, Dobra, Kupa (1 sarcophagus) Rivers
properties.UNFINISHED_PRODUCTS: In the area between the Mrežnica and Kordun are scattered sarcophagi and urns in various stages of production. In Mraćin village is unfinished sarcophagus, still unattached form the rock.
properties.COMMENTS: In literature different number of quarries is documented: either 37 (Knezović 2012, 242) or as many as 29, which can be grouped into 11 clusters (Migotti 2018, 206).
properties.QUARRY_SITES: The surface quarries in the karst area of Kordun.

properties.NAME: Moslovačka Gora
properties.MATERIAL: Limestone, area rich of granite, amphibolite, gneiss as well

properties.NAME: Ravna Gora – Vinica quarries
properties.LOCATION: Near the villages of Vinica and Marčan, Croatia
properties.MATERIAL: Yellow-greyish exploitable stone: porous and soft limestone (vinicit) and macro and megaporous Lithotamnion limestone
properties.ARCHAEOLOGICAL_EVIDENCE: No archaeological evidence. In literature »stone of this area was exploited in ancient times«.

properties.NAME: Ruopanec
properties.MATERIAL: Greyish and yellow-greyish, porous and soft limestone
properties.ARCHAEOLOGICAL_EVIDENCE: No archaeological evidence. In literature »old quarries with the remnants of block extraction«.

properties.NAME: Mount Kalnik
properties.LOCATION: Several km to the south of Varaždinske toplice, Kalnik, Croatia
properties.POTENTIAL_TRANSPORT: Land routes, Bednja River (tributary of the Drava)

properties.NAME: Bucova
properties.LOCATION: Northeastern part of Bosnia and Herzegovina (entity Republika Srpska)
properties.BIBLIOGRAPHY: Téglás 1889; Müller et al. 1999; Müller et al. 2012; Russell 2013c.
properties.EXTRACTION_TRACES: Thin parallel extraction marks can be observed on the walls.
properties.MEASUREMENTS: The old quarry is located on the left bank of the Bistra River at an elevation of +850 m. The extraction face, approximately 17 km long, has a height of about 20 m. While the quarry has preserved Roman traces, it was functional between the 1980 and 1985, and again after 2000, thus the measurements do not correspond to Roman exploitation only. According to Téglás (1889), the wall of a Roman quarry had a width of 20 m and a height of 30 m.

properties.NAME: Motajica
properties.MATERIAL: Slate granite (yellowish stone with grey granulate additions)
properties.POTENTIAL_TRANSPORT: Sava River; transport to Cibalae on lesser streams: Berava, Bosut, Studva, possible inland waterway 18crossed an artificial canal to the Vuka River

properties.NAME: Ozren or Konjuh
properties.POTENTIAL_TRANSPORT: Bosna River, Vrbas River, Sava River

properties.NAME: Ozren or Konjuh (Konjuh ***)
properties.POTENTIAL_TRANSPORT: Bosna River, Vrbas River, Sava River

properties.NAME: Ram
properties.LOCATION: Roman Lederata, modern Ram, some 15 km from Viminacium, Serbia

properties.NAME: Outside the NW corner of Felix Romuliana
Magura Hill
properties.LOCATION: 1 km SE of Felix Romuliana – Gamzigrad, Serbia
properties.MATERIAL: Volcaniclastic sandstone, Silty marly limestone – reddish and greenish
properties.EXTRACTION_TRACES: Wedge holes visible in the quarry.
properties.COMMENTS: Wedge holes visible also on a block from the fortification core.

properties.NAME: Rgotina area
properties.LOCATION: Rgotina, some 10 km NE of Felix Romuliana, Serbia
properties.PRODUCTS: Architectural elements (capitals, for the plinths of column bases), peristyle of palace, both mausolea, ...

properties.NAME: Rgotina area
properties.LOCATION: Some 10 km NE of Felix Romuliana, Serbia

properties.NAME: Hum
properties.LOCATION: Hum village, vicinity of Niš, Serbia

properties.NAME: Unknown – probably in Prishtina area (on map Gračanica)
properties.MATERIAL: Large grained marble with reddish veins

properties.NAME: Klecka
properties.ARCHAEOLOGICAL_EVIDENCE: Altar, funerary monument and head of statue found near Lipjan
properties.POTENTIAL_TRANSPORT: Bistrica River, tributary of the Morava River

properties.NAME: Kacanik
properties.LOCATION: Near Grlica, in the valley of the Lepanac river, Kosovo

properties.NAME: Ostrozub
properties.MATERIAL: Limestone of various qualities; one section in grey colour, the other white

properties.NAME: Shkoza
properties.POTENTIAL_TRANSPORT: Smaller rivers, but watershed of Morava river

properties.NAME: Shkoza near Decan
properties.POTENTIAL_TRANSPORT: Bistrica River, watershed of Morava River

properties.NAME: Svilare
properties.BIBLIOGRAPHY: Keramitčiev 1987; Mikulčić 1982, 78-79; Lilčić 2001, 121-124; Basotova 2019, 12 (reference to:  Keramitičiev 1987, 120).
properties.COMMENTS: Chemical analyses of artefacts from Scupi and its area confirmed this quarry, which is also mentioned as a quarry of travertine from the Roman period in the literature.
properties.QUARRY_SITES: Between Gorno Svilare and Dolno Svilare, also Kameno brdo location

properties.NAME: Govrlevo-Dračevica
properties.LOCATION: Skopje Valley,10 km SW of Skopje, North Macedonia,

properties.NAME: Četiri kamena
properties.LOCATION: Kumanovo Valley, by village Dimonce, North Macedonia
properties.SUPPLY: Scupi and settlements in Kumanovo and Skopje valleys
properties.ARCHAEOLOGICAL_EVIDENCE: Traces of ancient, likely Roman exploitation

properties.NAME: Rugince
properties.LOCATION: 5 km NE of Četiri kamena, North Macedonia
properties.SUPPLY: Scupi and settlements in Kumanovo and Skopje valleys

properties.NAME: Makreš
properties.LOCATION: NE of Kumanovo Valley, North Macedonia
properties.SUPPLY: Scupi and settlements in Kumanovo and Skopje valleys
properties.ARCHAEOLOGICAL_EVIDENCE: Visible ancient traces, but not necessarily Roman

properties.NAME: Vuksan
properties.LOCATION: Kumanovo Valley, village Matejče, North Macedonia,
properties.SUPPLY: Scupi and settlements in Kumanovo and Skopje valleys
properties.COMMENTS: No ancient extraction traces preserved due to modern exploitation.

properties.NAME: Lipkovo
properties.LOCATION: Kumanovo Valley, 11 km NW from Kumanovo, North Macedonia,
properties.SUPPLY: Scupi and settlements in Kumanovo and Skopje Valleys
properties.ARCHAEOLOGICAL_EVIDENCE: Traces probably obliterated by modern exploitation

properties.NAME: Sivec
properties.SUPPLY: Styberra, Heraclea Lyncestis, some in Stobi
properties.BIBLIOGRAPHY: Tomović 1993, 21; Russell 2013; Prochaska 2013; Niewohner et al. 2013.
properties.EXTRACTION_TRACES: Traces of Roman exploitation preserved on some blocks in the quarry area.
properties.COMMENTS: Presently exploited by the Mermeren Kombinat AD.

properties.NAME: Pletvar
properties.LOCATION: Near Prilep, 8 km away from Sivec, North Macedonia
properties.ARCHAEOLOGICAL_EVIDENCE: Traces of Roman exploitation, various finds: iron chisels, parts of architectural elements, unfinished products
properties.UNFINISHED_PRODUCTS: Bosses of architectural elements, unfinished products (reported by Babić 1972).
properties.EXTRACTION_TRACES: Several crater-shaped pits are good evidence of pre-modern quarrying. Below the western pit lie five square blocks, about 80 cm long and 40-60 cm wide, which were quarried with pickaxes and preserve tool marks.
properties.COMMENTS: Measurements of Roman exploitation are uncertain, but in Prochaska 2021: “Fist sized debris extends along the whole length of it [steep slope], over several hundred meters.”

properties.NAME: Unknown - probably close to Gradsko
properties.PRODUCTS: Stobi (seats of the theatre, heroon), statuary

properties.NAME: Pohorje
properties.BIBLIOGRAPHY: Djurić 1997; Russell 2013; Prochaska 2021; Djurić et al. 2025b.
properties.UNFINISHED_PRODUCTS: On a pathway below the slope of Motaln quarry was found a fragment of a half-finished marble vessel – mortar.
properties.EXTRACTION_TRACES: No visible tool-marks on the quarry walls, but parallel grooves made by pick are visible on a block found in the bed of a stream below the Motaln quarry. A marble block with characteristic grooved pick-marks was found in front of the entrance to one of the loci of the “Kresnik quarry”, dimensions 140 x 50 x 35 cm.
properties.MEASUREMENTS: Šmartno/Frajham Complex: most quarries located on the west slope of Pohorje Mountains with its upper edge at approximately 800 m asl and the valley floor at approximately 700 m, with an average gradient of 22° or 40 %. Marble extraction took place at several levels in a 230 m wide and 1.700 m long strip.
properties.COMMENTS:
At the Šmartno/Frajham Complex are remains of 66 limekilns, which are traces of modern, but possible even earlier marble extraction. An important find, probably from the area of Motaln quarry, is a votive altar with a depiction of a stonemason, identified as Saxanus. During the construction of the church of St. Martin in Šmartno (13th century) funerary and votive monuments were used, but also several half-finished products.
Presumed stonemasons’ settlement was found in the valley of the Velika Polskava.
properties.QUARRY_SITES: Hudinja, Šmartno/Frajham Complex (including Videc), Motaln, Vitanje

properties.NAME: Salla
properties.COMMENTS: No direct physical remnants of Roman quarrying techniques are clearly visible today.

properties.NAME: Gummern
properties.BIBLIOGRAPHY: Jantsch 1929; Kieslinger 1956; Djurić and Müller 2009; Russell 2013; Prochaska 2021.
properties.EXTRACTION_TRACES: In the first decades of the 20th century ancient traces were visible (Jantsch 1929; Kieslinger 1956). There are minor signs of ancient exploitation visible, specifically pick-traces, around another modern mines (Krastal), which is located a few km away from the Gummern quarry.
properties.COMMENTS: Because of the marble production and the enormous expansion of the mine site during the last decades, the Roman traces of marble mining were destroyed.

properties.NAME: Budakalász
properties.COMMENTS: Travertine in Budakalász still quarried today. The Roman exploitation is confirmed due to artefacts stone analysis.
properties.QUARRY_SITES: Buda Hills, Monalovác Hill and a smaller Roman quarry on Gellért Hill

properties.NAME: Berkovitsa
properties.LOCATION: Near the present-day town of Berkovitsa, Bulgaria
properties.BIBLIOGRAPHY: Braemer 2004; Russell 2013; Zagorchev 2014; Prochaska and Živić 2018; Andreeva et al. 2024.
properties.EXTRACTION_TRACES: Remnants of the Roman mining activity can still be found around Berkovitsa.
properties.COMMENTS: For an aerial photo from the region of Berkovitsa and a quarry façade, see Andreeva et al. 2024, Figs. 5a and 5b. More data is needed to determine the scope, scale and chronology of the marble exploitation at Berkovitsa. The Berkovitsa quarry is still exploited today (modern quarry site 510 m above sea level, 20 km southwards of Montana.

properties.NAME: Ugljevik
properties.LOCATION: Northeastern part of Bosnia and Herzegovina (entity Republika Srpska)
